title: Neoporteria backebergii
taxonomy: Neoporteria backebergii is classified within the family Cactaceae, which includes all species of cacti. Further, it belongs to the subfamily Opuntioideae, a group of cacti characterized by flat or tree-like growth. The genus Neoporteria is often considered synonymous with the genus Eriosyce, which is a group of cacti native to Chile and Argentina. Within the Eriosyce genus, Neoporteria backebergii is often placed as a distinct species. However, there is ongoing debate and revision regarding the classification and taxonomy of this species.

intro: Neoporteria backebergii, also known as the "backeberg's prickly pear cactus", is a species of cactus in the genus Neoporteria, native to the Atacama Desert in Chile. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic effects, making it useful for wound healing and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Neoporteria backebergii include flavonoids, terpenoids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, this plant is often made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, with the most common preparations being a tea to aid digestion and a salve to promote wound healing.
